summary: Iter20 CLOSED ‚Äî ESLint regression from iter19 is fully resolved. All 11 `eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` directives are restored across 7 files with justifying inline comments (spot-checked ChatBox.jsx:53 'loadHistory new closure every render', AdminDashboard.jsx:47 'nav stable' + :203 'reload closure', ArtistDashboard.jsx:75 'refresh()', BookingFlow.jsx:73, CustomerDashboard.jsx:26, Search.jsx:87, admin/AdminEnterprise.jsx:15/203/472/526 ‚Äî every directive has a preceding // comment). `yarn build` in production mode returns `Compiled successfully.` with 0 warnings; live CRA dev server tail on /var/log/supervisor/frontend.out.log also shows `Compiled successfully!` / `webpack compiled successfully`. All iter19 wins intact: (a) email_service.py has `import secrets` at line 8, ZERO `import random` references, `secrets.randbelow(900000)+100000` at line 40; (b) 19/19 test_iter19_secure_otp.py assertions pass ‚Äî mock-mode returns '123456', live-mode 100 unique OTPs, full send‚Üíverify‚Üíregister round-trip 200+token, all iter11-18 regression endpoints green (chat/access paid+unpaid, search/ai, calendar.ics, my-bookings.csv, revenue.csv, wallet, admin/coupons, admin/kyc, reviews/artist); (c) grep insertAdjacentHTML in /app/frontend/src ‚Üí only 1 doc-comment match in ArtistDashboard.jsx:12, zero functional occurrences; DOM inspection on live pages returned false for insertAdjacentHTML; (d) empty `catch(){}` grep on auth.jsx + ArtistDashboard.jsx ‚Üí 0 matches, both `console.warn` observability logs present at auth.jsx:45 and ArtistDashboard.jsx:272. UI smoke on live public URL: artist priya@ login ‚Üí /artist dashboard renders (57 bookings, 41 pending, 8638 views, 0 console errors); customer login ‚Üí /customer bookings shows 6√ó 'üí¨ Chat' + 11√ó 'üîí Pay to Unlock Chat' labels correctly (matches paid/unpaid state). No regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: All 11 restored eslint-disable directives now have a preceding one-line justification (e.g. '`loadHistory` is a new closure every render; including it loops.'). This is the correct fix ‚Äî CRA's react-hooks/exhaustive-deps rule is legitimately noisy for the in-render `load`/`refresh`/`run` closure pattern; the disable+comment is the community-standard resolution. Long-term: extract these closures with useCallback so the dep is stable and the directive can be removed, but that's a refactor, not a bug.

iter19 findings still stand: ArtistDashboard.jsx is 1087 lines (candidate for splitting MediaThumb, counter-offer modal, wallet card, KYC block); make_router at 779 lines flagged for future refactor ‚Äî explicitly deferred per iter20 scope note.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (yarn build clean, 0 warnings; live UI smoke green)

test_credentials: admin@booktalent.com/Admin@123, customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123, priya@booktalent.com/Artist@123 ‚Äî all validated from /app/memory/test_credentials.md
seed_data_creation: Only the throwaway user created by test_iter19_secure_otp.py::test_register_new_user (email pattern test_iter19_<epoch>@example.com) ‚Äî safe, prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter20 closes iter19 cleanly. `yarn build` = 0 warnings, `pytest test_iter19_secure_otp.py` = 19/19 pass, UI smoke on artist+customer routes green. If a future iter tries another eslint `--fix` sweep, remember: the 11 exhaustive-deps directives are INTENTIONAL and each has a justifying // comment above it ‚Äî do not strip them without a proper useCallback refactor. Deferred architectural items (make_router split, localStorage‚Üícookies auth, nested ternaries, `is`‚Üî`==` in tests) are noted in the problem statement as explicit non-issues.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of iter19 regression: ESLint 9 stand-alone treated the 11 exhaustive-deps directives as unused-directives (no eslint.config.js at project root), but CRA's built-in react-hooks plugin actually fires them under `craco start`. Iter20 fix (by main agent): restored every directive along with a one-line explanatory comment describing why the missing dep is intentional (in-render closure that would infinite-loop OR react-router `nav` which is stable). Verified: `yarn build` production compile now returns exactly `Compiled successfully.` with 0 warnings; CRA dev server tail also shows `webpack compiled successfully` with no exhaustive-deps output.
